summary: Completed all 8 steps in TDD order. Created dashboard/src/dashboard/data/memory.py with 3 functions: mcp_tool_call (generic JSON-RPC transport), get_memory_status (get_status tool with offline fallback), get_queue_stats (get_queue_stats tool with offline fallback). Hardened mcp_tool_call against malformed responses (missing content, invalid JSON, empty arrays). All 12 tests pass across 4 test classes: TestMcpToolCall (3), TestGetMemoryStatus (3), TestGetQueueStats (3), TestMalformedResponse (3). Used httpx.MockTransport for all test mocking.

Completed review-requested fixes (steps 9-10). Step 9: wrote 3 failing tests exposing gaps — ValueError from non-200 not caught in get_memory_status/get_queue_stats, and JSONDecodeError from non-JSON body not caught in mcp_tool_call. Step 10: fixed all 3 issues — added ValueError to except clauses in both get_* functions, wrapped resp.json() in try/except in mcp_tool_call. All 15 tests pass. All plan steps are now done.

Completed all 8 steps + prerequisite for task 21 (reconciliation panel). Created templates/partials/ directory, implemented timeago filter function in app.py with Jinja2 registration, implemented GET /partials/recon route with asyncio.gather for 5 concurrent data queries, and created recon.html partial template with two-column grid layout (left: buffer stats, burst state, watermarks, verdict cards; right: recent runs table). All empty-state fallbacks work correctly. Fixed test approach: used ExitStack context managers instead of @patch decorators (incompatible with pytest fixtures), and sentinel-based defaults in helper to properly pass None. All 66 tests pass across 4 test files.
